fields.title: Fix invalid tool names in TaskRegistry builtins
fields.description:
TaskRegistry.builtins() references tools that don't exist: ls, find, grep.

Actual available tools: read, write, edit, bash, bd, task, web_search_exa, crawling_exa, get_code_context_exa, git_commit_with_user_approval

Current broken config:
- search: tools: ['read', 'ls', 'find', 'grep']  
- review: tools: ['read', 'ls', 'find', 'grep', 'bash']
- planning: tools: ['read', 'ls', 'find', 'grep']

Should be:
- search: tools: ['read', 'bash'] (or just read if truly read-only)
- review: tools: ['read', 'bash']
- planning: tools: ['read', 'bash'] (or just read)

Note: read-only enforcement should be via skill guidance, not fake tool restrictions.

Related: tau-8oj (silent ignore of non-existent tools)
fields.close_reason: Already fixed: extensions/task/src/registry.ts builtins use tools ['read','bash'] for search/review/planning (no ls/find/grep). No further changes needed.
